proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one of the 35 anonymized features has a non-zero univariate association with objective_response in the 50,000-patient cohort.

analyses.code: for c in features: chi2/pointbiserial vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: Univariate screen of all 35 features. Strongest associations (smallest p): feature_004 (ordinal 0/1/2, p=1.8e-90, higher value -> lower response), feature_014 (binary, p=5.8e-70, +6.7pp), feature_001 (binary, p=1.9e-55, +5.6pp), feature_021 (binary, p=6.6e-51, +5.4pp), feature_020 (continuous, p=8e-31, r=-0.052), feature_024 (continuous, p=2.2e-16, r=-0.037), feature_029 (p=2.8e-7, r=+0.023), feature_026 (binary, p=3.3e-6, -2.3pp), feature_027 (continuous, p=6e-6, r=-0.020), feature_009 (binary, p=3.5e-5, -2.4pp). Many features (feature_011 [age 18-92], feature_010, feature_005, feature_011, feature_028, feature_023, feature_002, feature_017) had no detectable main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of the ordinal variable feature_004 (0/1/2) are associated with a lower probability of objective_response (monotone gradient across all three levels).

analyses.result_summary: Response: 23.1% at feature_004=0, 17.8% at feature_004=1, 11.6% at feature_004=2 (chi-square p<<1e-90). Adjusted logistic regression coefficient = -0.36 per unit (OR 0.70, p=9e-93). Monotone-decreasing as hypothesized.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001=1 is associated with higher objective_response rate than feature_001=0.
feature_014=1 is associated with higher objective_response rate than feature_014=0.
feature_021=1 is associated with higher objective_response rate than feature_021=0.

analyses.result_summary: Response 22.1% with feature_001=1 vs 16.5% with feature_001=0; chi-square p=1.9e-55. Marginal logistic OR=1.45.
Response 23.5% with feature_014=1 vs 16.7% with feature_014=0; p=5.8e-70. Marginal logistic OR=1.55.
Response 22.0% with feature_021=1 vs 16.6% with feature_021=0; p=6.6e-51. Marginal logistic OR=1.44.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of feature_020 (continuous, range 0-23.6) are associated with lower objective_response.
Higher values of feature_024 (continuous, range 20-100) are associated with lower objective_response.
Higher values of feature_029 (continuous, range 1.7-5.5) are associated with higher objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Logistic coefficient -0.0367 per unit feature_020 (OR 0.964, p=8e-31). Negative as hypothesized.
Logistic coefficient -0.0052 per unit feature_024 (OR 0.995, p=1.2e-17). Negative as hypothesized.
Logistic coefficient +0.122 per unit feature_029 (OR 1.13, p=1.4e-7). Positive as hypothesized.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for all 35 features simultaneously, the same set of features (feature_004 negative, feature_001/014/021 positive, feature_020/024 negative, feature_029 positive, feature_026/009 negative) remain independently significant predictors of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Multivariable logistic regression confirms: feature_004 coef -0.36 (p=9e-93), feature_014 +0.44 (p=2e-71), feature_001 +0.37 (p=2e-57), feature_021 +0.36 (p=8e-54), feature_020 -0.037 (p=3e-31), feature_024 -0.005 (p=1e-17), feature_029 +0.12 (p=1e-7), feature_026 -0.17 (p=6e-7), feature_009 -0.19 (p=4e-6), feature_027 -0.006 (p=6e-6). All other features non-significant after adjustment, including feature_011 (age, p=0.67), feature_010 (p=0.34), and electrolyte-like features (feature_023, _002, _017, all p>0.7). Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001, feature_014, and feature_021 are statistically independent of one another (joint distribution = product of marginals), implying their individual main effects on response are additive rather than driven by overlap.

analyses.result_summary: Marginals: feature_001=0.401, feature_014=0.300, feature_021=0.400. Observed pairwise cell sizes match independence to within 1% (e.g. f001=1 & f014=1 = 5,986; expected under independence ~ 50000*0.401*0.300 = 6,015). Three-way co-occurrence observed = 2,401 (4.80%) vs expected 0.401*0.300*0.400*50000 = 2,406. The three indicators are essentially independent in the cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the joint distribution of (feature_001, feature_014, feature_021), response rate is approximately constant across all eight combinations -- i.e. the three main effects are simply additive.
ALTERNATIVE: Patients with all three of feature_001=1 AND feature_014=1 AND feature_021=1 simultaneously have a response rate substantially higher than the additive prediction from main effects.

analyses.result_summary: Striking three-way AND-interaction discovered. Response rate by combination: (0,0,0)=17.2% n=12533; (0,0,1)=16.2% n=8383; (0,1,0)=16.0% n=5404; (0,1,1)=15.7% n=3624; (1,0,0)=16.4% n=8458; (1,0,1)=16.9% n=5612; (1,1,0)=16.0% n=3585; (1,1,1)=63.3% n=2401. Seven of the eight cells lie within 16-17%; only the all-three-positive cell jumps to 63.3% -- a 47-percentage-point excess that is not predicted by main effects. Hypothesis h7.1 refuted; h7.2 strongly supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a logistic regression of objective_response on feature_001, feature_014, feature_021 and their two-way and three-way product terms, the three-way interaction coefficient is positive and significant (and dominates), while the main effects and two-way interactions are near zero.

analyses.result_summary: Coefficients: f001 -0.05 (p=0.17), f014 -0.09 (p=0.05), f021 -0.07 (p=0.07), f001:f014 +0.05 (p=0.46), f001:f021 +0.10 (p=0.09), f014:f021 +0.05 (p=0.50), three-way f001:f014:f021 = +2.13 (OR 8.4, p<1e-90, z=20.4). Hypothesis supported: the response signal is captured entirely by the three-way AND term.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Response rate increases monotonically with the number of (feature_001, feature_014, feature_021) markers set to 1 (a dose-response with 0, 1, 2, 3 positives).
ALTERNATIVE: Response is flat across counts 0, 1, 2 and only rises at count=3 (a step function consistent with a strict logical AND).

analyses.result_summary: Response by count: 0 -> 17.2% (n=12533), 1 -> 16.2% (n=22245), 2 -> 16.3% (n=12821), 3 -> 63.3% (n=2401). Flat across 0-2, jumps sharply only at count=3. h9.1 refuted, h9.2 supported -- the relationship is a logical conjunction, not a graded dose response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The marginal effect of each of feature_001, feature_014, feature_021 on response is essentially zero in every stratum where the other two are not BOTH equal to 1; the marginal effect becomes large and positive only in the stratum where the other two are both 1.

analyses.code: for each of the 3, stratify by other 2
analyses.result_summary: Effect of feature_001 across (f014,f021) strata: -0.7pp (0,0), +0.7pp (0,1), 0pp (1,0), +47.6pp (1,1). Same pattern symmetrically for feature_014 and feature_021 (each +46-48pp only when the other two are both 1, otherwise within +/-1pp). The three indicators are functionally symmetric and each is strictly necessary. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the triple-positive subgroup (n=2401, baseline response 63.3%), at least one binary feature substantially suppresses (lowers) the response rate when set to 1.

analyses.code: for each feature: chi2 vs response within triple+
analyses.result_summary: Two binary features dramatically suppress response within triple+: feature_026 (response 71.7% if 0 vs 18.4% if 1, n=2025/376, p=7.6e-86) and feature_009 (response 69.1% if 0 vs 18.8% if 1, n=2125/276, p=3.3e-59). When BOTH suppressors are absent (feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0), response is 78.6% (n=1790). Their joint absence/presence pattern within triple+: (0,0) 78.6% n=1790, (0,1) 18.7% n=235, (1,0) 18.2% n=335, (1,1) 19.5% n=41. Either suppressor alone abolishes the triple+ benefit. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_026 has a negative interaction with the triple-positive indicator (feature_001=1 AND feature_014=1 AND feature_021=1) such that, in the presence of feature_026=1, the triple-positive benefit is fully abolished.

analyses.result_summary: triple coef = +2.55 (p<<1e-100), feature_026 main = +0.02 (n.s. on log-odds scale), triple:feature_026 interaction = -2.44 (p=2e-62). The interaction approximately cancels the triple effect. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_009 has a negative interaction with the triple-positive indicator such that, in the presence of feature_009=1, the triple-positive benefit is fully abolished.

analyses.result_summary: triple coef = +2.42 (p<<1e-100), feature_009 main = -0.005 (n.s.), triple:feature_009 = -2.26 (p=4e-42). The interaction approximately cancels the triple effect. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_004 (the strong univariate negative prognostic) is itself modified by the triple-positive subgroup -- i.e. there is a triple x feature_004 interaction.

analyses.result_summary: Main effects: triple +2.15 (p<<1e-100), feature_004 -0.39 (p<<1e-90). Interaction triple:feature_004 = +0.063 (p=0.34, NOT significant). feature_004 acts as a stable main-effect prognostic that lowers response by ~9pp per unit both inside and outside the triple-positive subgroup, but does not cancel the triple effect. Hypothesis (of interaction) refuted; feature_004 is purely additive.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The high-response subgroup is fully defined by the conjunction (feature_001=1 AND feature_014=1 AND feature_021=1 AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0); within this subgroup the response rate exceeds 75%.

analyses.result_summary: Refined subgroup: 1,790 patients (3.58% of cohort). Response rate = 78.6% vs 16.5% in the complement (chi-square p<<1e-300; risk difference +62.1pp). Subgroup definition is supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the refined high-response subgroup, no further binary or continuous feature reliably predicts response (i.e. the three positives plus the two suppressors capture the bulk of the heterogeneity).
ALTERNATIVE: Within the refined subgroup, feature_004 (ordinal 0/1/2) further stratifies response (lower feature_004 -> higher response).

analyses.code: for each feature: test vs response within refined subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Within the refined subgroup (n=1790), only two variables show notable signal: feature_004 (p=4.3e-10; response 85.9% at level 0 [n=617], 77.2% at level 1 [n=902], 66.8% at level 2 [n=271]) and feature_020 (continuous, r=-0.084, p=4e-4). All other binary features p>0.1; all other continuous features p>0.07. h16.1 partially refuted; h16.2 supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The strictest high-response subgroup is (feature_001=1 AND feature_014=1 AND feature_021=1 AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0 AND feature_004=0); within this subgroup response exceeds 80%.

analyses.result_summary: Strictest subgroup: 617 patients (1.23% of cohort), response 85.9% vs 17.9% in complement; risk difference +68.0pp; OR ~30. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment-like effect of the triple-positive marker (response gain over non-triple) is modified by feature_011 (age 18-92): older patients gain less (or more) than younger patients.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(response ~ triple + feature_011 + triple:feature_011); also stratify by age group
analyses.result_summary: Stratified RDs: <60: +48.8pp; 60-69: +47.0pp; 70-79: +45.3pp; >=80: +45.8pp. Logistic interaction triple:feature_011 coef = -0.0036 per year (p=0.38, n.s.). The triple-positive benefit is essentially constant across the entire 18-92 age range. Hypothesis refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the remaining binary features (feature_005, feature_010, feature_015, feature_016, feature_018, feature_019, feature_025, feature_006, feature_008, feature_030, feature_032), at least one further modifies the triple-positive effect (interaction p<0.01).

analyses.code: for each: sm.Logit(response ~ triple + feat + triple:feat); within refined subgroup chi2
analyses.result_summary: None of the remaining binary features yields a significant triple-interaction or a within-refined-subgroup chi-square at p<0.05 after the suppressor adjustment (smallest p ~0.12 for feature_015 and feature_019). Hypothesis refuted -- no further binary modifier of treatment effect identified.

proposed_hypotheses.text: An exhaustive screen of all C(16,2)=120 pairwise AND combinations among the 16 binary features uncovers an alternative high-response combination (response >= 50%) outside the (feature_001, feature_014, feature_021) family.

analyses.result_summary: Top pairwise AND combos (by response in cell with both=1): feature_001 & feature_014 -> 34.9%; feature_014 & feature_021 -> 34.7%; feature_001 & feature_021 -> 30.8%; all other pairs <25%. The elevated values for the three pairs above are entirely driven by the triple-positive sub-cells they contain. No alternative high-response binary AND-combination exists. Hypothesis refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one continuous feature (other than feature_011/age, already tested) modifies the triple-positive treatment-like effect.

analyses.code: for each continuous feat: sm.Logit(response ~ triple + feat + triple:feat); also r within refined subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Tested feature_022, feature_024, feature_027, feature_020, feature_029, feature_034, feature_028, feature_007, feature_012, feature_031, feature_013, feature_033, feature_003, feature_035, feature_023, feature_002, feature_017. None showed a triple-interaction at p<0.01. Within the refined subgroup, only feature_020 reached r=-0.084 (p=4e-4); it appears to be a weak prognostic that operates similarly inside and outside the triple subgroup rather than a true effect modifier. Hypothesis effectively refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for the strongest continuous prognostics (feature_020, feature_024, feature_022, feature_029, feature_027) and ordinal feature_004, the triple-positive interaction with the suppressors (feature_026, feature_009) remains the dominant signal, with triple coef > +2.5 and suppressor interactions < -2.5.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(response ~ triple + feature_026 + feature_009 + triple:f026 + triple:f009 + feature_004 + feature_020 + feature_024 + feature_022 + feature_029 + feature_027 + feature_011)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted model: triple +2.98 (p<<1e-100), triple:feature_026 -2.75 (p=8e-74), triple:feature_009 -2.62 (p=4e-52), feature_004 -0.39 (p<<1e-90), feature_020 -0.040 (p=8e-35), feature_024 -0.0056 (p=1e-18), feature_022 -0.0021 (p=3e-5), feature_029 +0.130 (p=7e-8), feature_027 -0.0074 (p=7e-7), feature_011 (age) -0.0002 (p=0.85). The triple x suppressor interactions remain the dominant terms after full adjustment. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among non-triple-positive patients (n=47,599), there exists a definable subgroup (any combination of features) with response > 30%.

analyses.code: Among non-triple subjects: best subgroup search across feature_004 levels and pairwise AND combos
analyses.result_summary: Best stratum among non-triple: feature_004=0 -> response 20.8% (n=16,762); feature_004=1 -> 15.2% (n=23,754); feature_004=2 -> 10.8% (n=7,083). No combination of features outside the (feature_001, feature_014, feature_021) triple yields response > 25% in any stratum of >100 patients. Hypothesis refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treating any single one of feature_001, feature_014, feature_021 as the 'treatment' (with the other two as biomarkers): the treatment effect is concentrated in the subgroup where (other two = 1 AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0). Quantitatively: within (other two=1 AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0), the on-treatment response exceeds the off-treatment response by >50 percentage points.

analyses.code: Within feature_014=1 AND feature_021=1 AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0: compare feature_001=1 vs 0
analyses.result_summary: Within (feature_014=1 AND feature_021=1 AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0): feature_001=1 response = 78.6% (n=1790) vs feature_001=0 response = 15.8% (n=2789); risk difference = +62.8pp (p<<1e-300). By symmetry the same risk difference is recovered when feature_014 or feature_021 is treated as the index variable. The treatment-effect heterogeneity is therefore fully captured by: TREATMENT_EFFECT(any of f001/f014/f021) is large (+60-65pp) ONLY in the subgroup where (the other two of {f001,f014,f021} are both 1 AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0); essentially zero everywhere else. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT HETEROGENEITY HYPOTHESIS: Objective response is dramatically increased (response 78.6% vs ~16% baseline; absolute risk difference +62 percentage points; OR ~20) only in the subgroup defined by the conjunction (feature_001=1 AND feature_014=1 AND feature_021=1 AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0). Within this subgroup, response is further graded by feature_004 (response 85.9% at f004=0, 77.2% at f004=1, 66.8% at f004=2). All three of feature_001/014/021 must be present (none alone or in pairs is sufficient); both of feature_026/009 must be absent (either one suppresses the entire benefit). Treatment-effect heterogeneity is NOT modified by feature_011 (age), feature_010, feature_005, or any other binary or continuous feature in the dataset.

analyses.code: Final summary: subgroup definition + risk differences across all directions tested
analyses.result_summary: Final subgroup: feature_001=1 AND feature_014=1 AND feature_021=1 AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0. n=1790 (3.58% of cohort). Response in subgroup: 78.6%; response outside: 16.5%; absolute risk difference +62.1pp; chi-square p<<1e-300; OR ~19. Effect is symmetric across the three positives (each is necessary), is fully suppressed by either feature_026=1 or feature_009=1, is graded further by feature_004, and is invariant to age (feature_011) and to all other features tested. This is the best-supported treatment-by-biomarker subgroup hypothesis in the cohort.
